steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: "...55 USD and 137 USD in fellow OECD ______ and Switzerland, respectively." The blank fills "nations Costa Rica" — "Costa Rica" is a restrictive appositive naming which OECD nation costs 55 USD. The structure is: noun + restrictive appositive, which takes no punctuation.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — nations: Costa Rica: "...in fellow OECD nations: Costa Rica and Switzerland." A colon introduces a list or explanation, but here "Costa Rica and Switzerland" is part of the sentence structure as objects, not a listed elaboration. ✗
  • Choice B — nations Costa Rica: "...in fellow OECD nations Costa Rica and Switzerland." No punctuation — correct for restrictive appositives identifying which specific nations. ✓
  • Choice C — nations, Costa Rica: "...in fellow OECD nations, Costa Rica and Switzerland." A comma sets off a non-restrictive appositive, implying these are the only OECD nations — contradicted by the passage (38 members). ✗
  • Choice D — nations; Costa Rica: "...in fellow OECD nations; Costa Rica and Switzerland." A semicolon joins two independent clauses, but "Costa Rica and Switzerland, respectively" is not an independent clause. ✗
That is the correct answer.
